more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Zimbabwe’s beauty queen-turned-entrepreneur on what it takes to build a fragrance brand in Africa
more.description: Forbes Africa Brooke Bruk-Jackson did not set out to ruffle feathers. She was working in a beauty salon in Harare, Zimbabwe, in 2023, when a stranger handed her a piece of paper with a question: ‘What makes Zimbabwe unique?’ “The people,” she replied. more.title: Implats faces currency challenges in Zimbabwe
more.description: Times Live Impala Platinum (Implats) says it is navigating the perceived risk of operating in Zimbabwe as it scrambles to pay its suppliers and employees on time under the country’s foreign currency rules. Zimbabwean exporters can retain only 70 per cent of foreign earnings, while 30 per cent must be surrendered to the central bank [...]The post Implats faces currency challenges in Zimbabwe appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com.

more.title: Cricketer Kelis Ndhlovu out of hospital after being struck by bouncer
more.description: Allied Media Zimbabwean cricketer Kelis Ndhlovu is out of hospital and recovering well after being struck on the head by a bouncer during the second ODI against the White Ferns in Dunedin yesterday. more.title: Zanu PF Harare Province gives thumbs up to Constitutional Amendment Bill Number 3 to stretch Mnangagwa’s incumbency
more.description: ZANU PF Harare Province has endorsed the proposed Constitutional Amendment Bill Number 3 to extend President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s incumbency beyond 2028 when his second and final term expires. The ruling party’s Harare Provincial Coordinating Committee (PCC) members will soon convene inter-district meetings to explain the proposed amendments to party supporters to get a buy-in. Addressing [...]The post Zanu PF Harare Province gives thumbs up to Constitutional Amendment Bill Number 3 to stretch Mnangagwa’s incumbency appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com.

more.description: Source: Vital Chitungwiza dam project in limbo – The Standard Construction of the long-awaited Muda Dam, a strategic reservoir to resolve the chronic water shortages in Chitungwiza, has not yet commenced due to funding constraints, Parliament has heard. more.title: Puma says fuel supply stable, despite external price pressures
more.description: PUMA Energy, one of Zimbabwe’s largest petroleum marketers in the country, says fuel availability remains stable despite the Middle East turbulence, which has sent global oil prices sharply higher. Made in Zimbabwe branding The Middle East war has increased market volatility and pushed up fuel costs worldwide. Zimbabwe’s annual fuel consumption surged to over 2,1 [...]The post Puma says fuel supply stable, despite external price pressures appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Kavango Resources raises US$8.4m in UK and Zimbabwe to boost gold operations
more.description: London-listed Kavango Resources plc has raised approximately US$8.4 million through subscriptions in the UK and Zimbabwe to fund the expansion of its gold production operations, including the Hillside Gold Project, while pursuing the acquisition of the Nara Gold Project despite the seller’s recent default on completion terms.The post Kavango Resources raises US$8.4m in UK and Zimbabwe to boost gold operations appeared first on Nehanda Radio.
